   273→    if [[ -n "$tool_error" ]]; then
   274→        process_error "$tool_error"
   275→        tool_error_handled=true
   276→    fi
   277→
   278→    case "$msg_type" in
   279→        assistant)
   280→            # Process content blocks
   281→            local block_count
   282→            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length')
   283→
   284→            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
   285→                local block block_type
   286→                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]")

   289→                case "$block_type" in
   290→                    text)
   291→                        local text
   292→                        text=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.text // ""')
   293→                        process_text "$text"
   294→                        ;;
   295→                    tool_use)
   296→                        local tool_name tool_input
   297→                        tool_name=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.name // "unknown"')
   298→                        tool_input=$(echo "$block" | jq -c '.input // {}')
   299→                        process_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
   300→                        ;;
   301→                    thinking)
   302→                        local thinking
   303→                        thinking=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.thinking // ""')
   304→                        process_thinking "$thinking"
   305→                        ;;
   306→                esac
   307→            done
   308→            ;;
   309→
   310→        user)
   311→            # Check for tool result errors in user messages
   312→            # Skip if already handled via tool_use_result field
   313→            [[ "$tool_error_handled" == "true" ]] && return
   314→
   315→            if [[ "$content" == "null" || -z "$content" ]]; then
   316→                return
   317→            fi
   318→
   319→            local block_count
   320→            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length' 2>/dev/null) || return
   321→
   322→            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
   323→                local block block_type is_error
   324→                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]" 2>/dev/null) || continue
   325→                block_type=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.type // ""')
   326→
   327→                if [[ "$block_type" == "tool_result" ]]; then
   328→                    is_error=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
   329→                    if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
   330→                        local error_content
   331→                        error_content=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.content // "Tool error"' | head -n 1)
   332→                        process_error "$error_content"
   333→                    fi
   334→                fi
   335→            done
   336→            ;;
   337→
   338→        error)
   339→            local error_msg
   340→            error_msg=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.error.message // .error // .message // "Unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
   341→            process_error "$error_msg"
   342→            ;;
   343→
   344→        system|result)
   345→            # System messages - show if they have meaningful content
   346→            local result_text
   347→            result_text=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.result // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null)
   348→            [[ -n "$result_text" ]] && process_text "$result_text"
   349→            ;;
   350→
   351→        # Skip internal/lifecycle events silently
   352→        message_start|message_delta|message_stop|content_block_start|content_block_delta|content_block_stop)
   353→            ;;
   354→
   355→        *)
   356→            # Unknown type - skip silently unless debug
   357→            ;;
   358→    esac
   359→
   360→    # Periodic summary
   361→    check_summary_needed
   362→}
   363→
   364→# =============================================================================
   365→# MAIN
   366→# =============================================================================
   367→
   368→main() {
   369→    # Check for jq
   370→    if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
   371→        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - passing through raw output" >&2
   372→        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} Install: brew install jq (macOS) / apt install jq (Linux)" >&2
   373→        cat
   374→        return 0
   375→    fi
   376→
   377→    # Trap for clean exit with summary
   378→    trap 'echo ""; print_summary; exit 130' INT TERM
   379→
   380→    # Process stdin line by line
   381→    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
   382→        process_line "$line"
   383→    done
   384→
   385→    # Final summary
   386→    print_summary
   387→}
   388→
   389→# Run if executed directly (not sourced)
   390→if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
   391→    main "$@"
   392→fi
   393→

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# format-output.sh - Human-readable output formatting for Ralph loop monitoring
# =============================================================================
# Transforms Claude CLI stream-json output into readable format.
#
# Usage:
#   claude -p ... --output-format=stream-json | ./format-output.sh
#   cat claude-output.json | ./format-output.sh
#
# Performance: Optimized for streaming - single jq call per line, cached timestamps
# =============================================================================

set -uo pipefail
# Note: -e intentionally omitted - parser errors should not kill the formatter

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
DIM='\033[2m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'

# Icons
ICON_TEXT="T"
ICON_TOOL=">"
ICON_THINK="."
ICON_TODO="L"
ICON_ERROR="E"
ICON_COMMIT="C"

# Configuration
MAX_LINE_WIDTH=${MAX_LINE_WIDTH:-120}
SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC=${SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC:-300}
RAW_OUTPUT_FILE=${RAW_OUTPUT_FILE:-}
SHOW_THINKING=${SHOW_THINKING:-false}

# State tracking
START_TIME=$(date +%s)
LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$START_TIME
LINE_COUNT=0
ERROR_COUNT=0
TOOL_COUNT=0
TEXT_COUNT=0
COMMIT_COUNT=0
TODO_COMPLETED=0
TODO_PENDING=0
TODO_IN_PROGRESS=0

# Timestamp cache (avoid calling date on every line)
LAST_TS_SEC=0
CACHED_TS=""

# =============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

timestamp() {
    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    if [[ $now -ne $LAST_TS_SEC ]]; then
        CACHED_TS=$(date '+[%H:%M]')
        LAST_TS_SEC=$now
    fi
    printf '%s' "$CACHED_TS"
}

truncate_line() {
    local text="$1"
    local max_len="${2:-$MAX_LINE_WIDTH}"
    local len=${#text}

    if [[ $len -gt $max_len ]]; then
        # UTF-8 safe truncation using parameter expansion with fallback
        printf '%s...' "${text:0:$((max_len - 3))}"
    else
        printf '%s' "$text"
    fi
}

format_elapsed() {
    local seconds="$1"
    local hours=$((seconds / 3600))
    local minutes=$(((seconds % 3600) / 60))
    local secs=$((seconds % 60))

    if [[ $hours -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf "%dh%02dm" "$hours" "$minutes"
    elif [[ $minutes -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf "%dm%02ds" "$minutes" "$secs"
    else
        printf "%ds" "$secs"
    fi
}

print_summary() {
    local now elapsed
    now=$(date +%s)
    elapsed=$((now - START_TIME))

    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE} $(format_elapsed "$elapsed") | Tools: $TOOL_COUNT | Text: $TEXT_COUNT | Errors: $ERROR_COUNT${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE} Commits: $COMMIT_COUNT | Todos: $TODO_IN_PROGRESS active, $TODO_COMPLETED done, $TODO_PENDING pending${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo ""

    LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$now
}

check_summary_needed() {
    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    if [[ $((now - LAST_SUMMARY_TIME)) -ge $SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC ]]; then
        print_summary
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# JQ FILTER - Extract all needed fields in one call
# =============================================================================

# This jq filter extracts everything we need from a line in a single pass
# Note: tool_use_result can be a string (error msg) or object, handle both
JQ_EXTRACT_FILTER='{
  type: .type,
  content: (if .message.content then .message.content else null end),
  tool_error: (
    if (.tool_use_result | type) == "object" and .tool_use_result.is_error
    then .tool_use_result.content
    elif (.tool_use_result | type) == "string" and (.tool_use_result | startswith("Error"))
    then .tool_use_result
    else null
    end
  )
}'

# =============================================================================
# MESSAGE PROCESSORS
# =============================================================================

process_text() {
    local text="$1"
    [[ -z "${text// /}" ]] && return

    TEXT_COUNT=$((TEXT_COUNT + 1))

    # Check for commit-related messages
    if [[ "$text" =~ (committed|commit[[:space:]]+[a-f0-9]{7,}|git[[:space:]]+commit|Co-Authored-By) ]]; then
        COMMIT_COUNT=$((COMMIT_COUNT + 1))
        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${GREEN}${ICON_COMMIT}${NC} ${GREEN}$(truncate_line "$text")${NC}"
    else
        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${ICON_TEXT} $(truncate_line "$text")"
    fi
}

process_tool_use() {
    local name="$1"
    local input_json="$2"

    TOOL_COUNT=$((TOOL_COUNT + 1))

    local description=""

    case "$name" in
        Read)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.file_path // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Read: ${description##*/}"  # basename only
            ;;
        Write)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.file_path // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Write: ${description##*/}"
            ;;
        Edit)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.file_path // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Edit: ${description##*/}"
            ;;
        Bash)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.description // (.command | .[0:50]) // "cmd"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="$ $description"
            ;;
        Glob)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.pattern // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Glob: $description"
            ;;
        Grep)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.pattern // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Grep: $description"
            ;;
        Task)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.description // "task"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Task: $description"
            ;;
        TodoWrite)
            # Extract todo counts - accumulate, don't overwrite
            local completed pending in_progress
            completed=$(echo "$input_json" | jq '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="completed")] | length' 2>/dev/null) || completed=0
            pending=$(echo "$input_json" | jq '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="pending")] | length' 2>/dev/null) || pending=0
            in_progress=$(echo "$input_json" | jq '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="in_progress")] | length' 2>/dev/null) || in_progress=0

            TODO_COMPLETED=$completed
            TODO_PENDING=$pending
            TODO_IN_PROGRESS=$in_progress

            echo -e "$(timestamp) ${YELLOW}${ICON_TODO}${NC} ${YELLOW}Todos: $in_progress active, $completed done, $pending pending${NC}"
            return
            ;;
        WebFetch|WebSearch)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.url // .query // "web"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="$name: $description"
            ;;
        mcp__playwright__*)
            description="Playwright: ${name#mcp__playwright__}"
            ;;
        *)
            description="$name"
            ;;
    esac

    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${CYAN}${ICON_TOOL}${NC} ${CYAN}$(truncate_line "$description" 100)${NC}"
}

process_thinking() {
    [[ "$SHOW_THINKING" != "true" ]] && return

    local thinking="$1"
    local first_line
    first_line=$(echo "$thinking" | head -n 1)
    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${DIM}${ICON_THINK} $(truncate_line "$first_line" 80)${NC}"
}

process_error() {
    local error_msg="$1"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${RED}${ICON_ERROR} ERROR: $error_msg${NC}" >&2
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN LINE PROCESSOR
# =============================================================================

process_line() {
    local line="$1"

    LINE_COUNT=$((LINE_COUNT + 1))

    # Save raw output if configured
    [[ -n "$RAW_OUTPUT_FILE" ]] && echo "$line" >> "$RAW_OUTPUT_FILE"

    # Skip empty lines
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && return

    # Quick rejection of non-JSON (faster than jq validation)
    [[ "${line:0:1}" != "{" ]] && { echo "$line"; return; }

    # Single jq call to extract type and validate
    local extracted
    extracted=$(echo "$line" | jq -c "$JQ_EXTRACT_FILTER" 2>/dev/null) || {
        echo "$line"  # Not valid JSON, pass through
        return
    }

    local msg_type content tool_error
    msg_type=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')
    content=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -c '.content // []')
    tool_error=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -r '.tool_error // empty')

    # Handle tool result errors (from top-level tool_use_result field)
    local tool_error_handled=false
    if [[ -n "$tool_error" ]]; then
        process_error "$tool_error"
        tool_error_handled=true
    fi

    case "$msg_type" in
        assistant)
            # Process content blocks
            local block_count
            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length')

            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
                local block block_type
                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]")
                block_type=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')

                case "$block_type" in
                    text)
                        local text
                        text=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.text // ""')
                        process_text "$text"
                        ;;
                    tool_use)
                        local tool_name tool_input
                        tool_name=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.name // "unknown"')
                        tool_input=$(echo "$block" | jq -c '.input // {}')
                        process_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
                        ;;
                    thinking)
                        local thinking
                        thinking=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.thinking // ""')
                        process_thinking "$thinking"
                        ;;
                esac
            done
            ;;

        user)
            # Check for tool result errors in user messages
            # Skip if already handled via tool_use_result field
            [[ "$tool_error_handled" == "true" ]] && return

            if [[ "$content" == "null" || -z "$content" ]]; then
                return
            fi

            local block_count
            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length' 2>/dev/null) || return

            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
                local block block_type is_error
                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]" 2>/dev/null) || continue
                block_type=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.type // ""')

                if [[ "$block_type" == "tool_result" ]]; then
                    is_error=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
                    if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
                        local error_content
                        error_content=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.content // "Tool error"' | head -n 1)
                        process_error "$error_content"
                    fi
                fi
            done
            ;;

        error)
            local error_msg
            error_msg=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.error.message // .error // .message // "Unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
            process_error "$error_msg"
            ;;

        system|result)
            # System messages - show if they have meaningful content
            local result_text
            result_text=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.result // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null)
            [[ -n "$result_text" ]] && process_text "$result_text"
            ;;

        # Skip internal/lifecycle events silently
        message_start|message_delta|message_stop|content_block_start|content_block_delta|content_block_stop)
            ;;

        *)
            # Unknown type - skip silently unless debug
            ;;
    esac

    # Periodic summary
    check_summary_needed
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Check for jq
    if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - passing through raw output" >&2
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} Install: brew install jq (macOS) / apt install jq (Linux)" >&2
        cat
        return 0
    fi

    # Trap for clean exit with summary
    trap 'echo ""; print_summary; exit 130' INT TERM

    # Process stdin line by line
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        process_line "$line"
    done

    # Final summary
    print_summary
}

# Run if executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# Ralph Wiggum Loop - Production Hardened Version 2.1
# =============================================================================
# Based on Geoffrey Huntley's ORIGINAL method (external loop, clean context)
# 
# CRITICAL FIXES v2.1:
# 1. Claude CLI timeout configuration (CRITICAL - prevents 2-min kill)
# 2. API timeout configuration
# 3. Retry logic for transient failures
# 4. Cross-platform date compatibility (Linux/macOS)
# 5. bc dependency fallback
# 6. Fixed variable scope issues
# 7. Improved error handling
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

# =============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"

# Source circuit breaker
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh" ]]; then
    source "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh"
    CB_ENABLED=true
else
    CB_ENABLED=false
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} circuit-breaker.sh not found - running without protection"
fi

# Ralph files
PROMPT_BUILD="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_build.md"
PROMPT_PLAN="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_plan.md"
PROMPT_PLAN_WORK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_plan_work.md"
AGENTS_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/AGENTS.md"
IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN="${SCRIPT_DIR}/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"

# Output formatter (optional, for human-readable output)
FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/scripts/format-output.sh"
PRETTY_OUTPUT=${PRETTY_OUTPUT:-true}

# Default model
DEFAULT_MODEL="opus"

# Rate limiting
MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR=${MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR:-9999999}
CALLS_FILE="/tmp/ralph-calls-$(date +%Y%m%d%H)"

# Output capture
OUTPUT_DIR="/tmp/ralph-outputs-$$"
mkdir -p "$OUTPUT_DIR"

# =============================================================================
# TIMEOUT CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL)
# =============================================================================
# Claude CLI has a default 2-minute timeout on bash commands.
# This will kill long-running builds, tests, and optimizer runs!
# Configure these environment variables to extend timeouts.
#
# Reference: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/5615
# =============================================================================

# Bash command timeout (ms) - default 30 minutes
export BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=${BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS:-1800000}
# Maximum bash timeout (ms) - default 2 hours
export BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS=${BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS:-7200000}
# API request timeout (ms) - default 10 minutes
export API_TIMEOUT_MS=${API_TIMEOUT_MS:-600000}
# MCP server startup timeout (ms) - default 30 seconds
export MCP_TIMEOUT=${MCP_TIMEOUT:-30000}
# Disable non-essential traffic (improves stability)
export CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC=${CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC:-1}

# Retry configuration for Claude CLI calls
MAX_RETRIES=${MAX_RETRIES:-3}
RETRY_DELAY=${RETRY_DELAY:-5}

# Completion signals to detect
COMPLETION_SIGNALS=(
    "ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE"
    "PLAN_COMPLETE"
    "EXIT_SIGNAL"
    "<promise>DONE</promise>"
    "<promise>COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>PLAN_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>FIX_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>RALPH_BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>BLOCKED</promise>"
)

# =============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

log_info() { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
log_success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"; }
log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
log_error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }

print_banner() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}"
    echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║     ██████╗  █████╗ ██╗     ██████╗ ██╗  ██╗                     ║"
    echo "║     ██╔══██╗██╔══██╗██║     ██╔══██╗██║  ██║                     ║"
    echo "║     ██████╔╝███████║██║     ██████╔╝███████║                     ║"
    echo "║     ██╔══██╗██╔══██║██║     ██╔═══╝ ██╔══██║                     ║"
    echo "║     ██║  ██║██║  ██║███████╗██║     ██║  ██║                     ║"
    echo "║     ╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚══════╝╚═╝     ╚═╝  ╚═╝                     ║"
    echo "║                                                                  ║"
    echo "║     Metro Fleet Mileage Management System                        ║"
    echo "║     Production-Hardened Ralph Loop v2.1                          ║"
    echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo -e "${NC}"
}

# Cross-platform date from timestamp
date_from_timestamp() {
    local timestamp="$1"
    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU date (Linux)
        date -d "@$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    else
        # BSD date (macOS)
        date -r "$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    fi
}

check_rate_limit() {
    if [[ -f "$CALLS_FILE" ]]; then
        local calls
        calls=$(cat "$CALLS_FILE")
        if [[ $calls -ge $MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR ]]; then
            log_error "Rate limit reached: $MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR calls/hour"
            log_info "Reset in: $(( 60 - $(date +%M) )) minutes"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    return 0
}

increment_call_count() {
    local current=0
    if [[ -f "$CALLS_FILE" ]]; then
        current=$(cat "$CALLS_FILE")
    fi
    echo $((current + 1)) > "$CALLS_FILE"
}

check_prerequisites() {
    if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "Claude CLI not found. Install: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-cli"
        exit 1
    fi

    if ! git rev-parse --git-dir &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "Not in a git repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$AGENTS_FILE" ]]; then
        log_error "AGENTS.md not found at: $AGENTS_FILE"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check for jq (required for circuit breaker)
    if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
        log_warn "jq not found - circuit breaker features limited"
        log_info "Install with: apt install jq (Linux) or brew install jq (macOS)"
    fi
    
    # Check for envsubst (required for plan-work mode)
    if ! command -v envsubst &> /dev/null; then
        log_warn "envsubst not found - plan-work mode may not work"
        log_info "Install with: apt install gettext-base (Linux) or brew install gettext (macOS)"
    fi
}

check_completion_signal() {
    local output_file="$1"

    for signal in "${COMPLETION_SIGNALS[@]}"; do
        # Case-insensitive matching (-i) for robustness
        if grep -qi "$signal" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "$signal"
            return 0
        fi
    done

    return 1
}

check_for_errors() {
    local output_file="$1"
    
    # Check for JSON error blocks
    if grep -q '"type":"error"' "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        grep '"type":"error"' "$output_file" | head -1
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for common error patterns
    if grep -qE "FATAL|CRITICAL|Exception|Traceback" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        grep -E "FATAL|CRITICAL|Exception|Traceback" "$output_file" | head -1
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for API timeout errors
    if grep -qE "timeout|ETIMEDOUT|Request timed out" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        grep -E "timeout|ETIMEDOUT|Request timed out" "$output_file" | head -1
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

push_changes() {
    local branch="$1"
    local max_retries=3
    local retry=0
    
    while [[ $retry -lt $max_retries ]]; do
        if git push origin "$branch" 2>&1; then
            return 0
        fi
        
        retry=$((retry + 1))
        
        if [[ $retry -eq 1 ]]; then
            log_warn "Push failed. Trying to create remote branch..."
            if git push -u origin "$branch" 2>&1; then
                return 0
            fi
        fi
        
        log_warn "Push attempt $retry/$max_retries failed. Retrying in 5s..."
        sleep 5
    done
    
    return 1
}

# Run Claude CLI with retry logic for transient failures
run_claude_with_retry() {
    local prompt_file="$1"
    local output_file="$2"
    local mode="$3"
    local retry=0
    local exit_code

    # Determine if we should use pretty output formatting
    local use_formatter=false
    if [[ "$PRETTY_OUTPUT" == "true" && -x "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT" ]]; then
        use_formatter=true
    fi

    while [[ $retry -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
        if [[ "$mode" == "plan-work" ]]; then
            if [[ "$use_formatter" == "true" ]]; then
                envsubst < "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file" | "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            else
                envsubst < "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            fi
        else
            if [[ "$use_formatter" == "true" ]]; then
                cat "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file" | "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            else
                cat "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            fi
        fi

        # Check for transient errors that should be retried
        if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
            return 0
        fi

        # Check if it's a retryable error
        if grep -qE "ETIMEDOUT|ECONNRESET|ECONNREFUSED|503|504|rate.?limit" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            retry=$((retry + 1))
            if [[ $retry -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
                log_warn "Transient error detected. Retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s... (attempt $((retry + 1))/$MAX_RETRIES)"
                sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
                continue
            fi
        fi

        # Non-retryable error or max retries reached
        return $exit_code
    done

    return 1
}

cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$OUTPUT_DIR"
    log_info "Cleaned up temporary files"
}

show_usage() {
    echo "Usage: ./loop.sh [MODE] [OPTIONS]"
    echo ""
    echo "Modes:"
    echo "  (none)              Build mode, unlimited iterations"
    echo "  <number>            Build mode, max N iterations"
    echo "  plan                Planning mode, unlimited"
    echo "  plan <number>       Planning mode, max N iterations"
    echo "  plan-work \"desc\"    Scoped planning for specific work"
    echo ""
    echo "Examples:"
    echo "  ./loop.sh                          # Build, unlimited"
    echo "  ./loop.sh 50                       # Build, max 50 iterations"
    echo "  ./loop.sh plan                     # Plan, unlimited"
    echo "  ./loop.sh plan 5                   # Plan, max 5 iterations"
    echo "  ./loop.sh plan-work \"ingestion\"    # Scoped plan"
    echo ""
    echo "Options:"
    echo "  -h, --help              Show this help"
    echo "  -m, --model MODEL       Use specific model (default: opus)"
    echo "  -v, --verbose           Verbose output"
    echo "  --no-push               Skip git push after iterations"
    echo "  --no-stop-on-complete   Continue even after completion signal"
    echo ""
    echo "Environment Variables:"
    echo "  MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR          Rate limit (default: 100)"
    echo "  CB_MAX_COST_USD             Circuit breaker cost limit (default: 50)"
    echo "  CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD    Iterations without commit (default: 3)"
    echo ""
    echo "Timeout Configuration (CRITICAL for long operations):"
    echo "  BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS     Bash command timeout (default: 1800000 = 30min)"
    echo "  BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS         Max bash timeout (default: 7200000 = 2hr)"
    echo "  API_TIMEOUT_MS              API request timeout (default: 600000 = 10min)"
    echo ""
    echo "Output:"
    echo "  PRETTY_OUTPUT               Enable formatted output (default: true)"
    echo "  --raw                       Disable output formatting (show raw JSON)"
    echo ""
    echo "Safety:"
    echo "  Ctrl+C                  Stop the loop"
    echo "  git reset --hard        Revert uncommitted changes"
    echo "  ./circuit-breaker.sh reset  Reset circuit breaker state"
}

# =============================================================================
# PARSE ARGUMENTS
# =============================================================================

MODE="build"
PROMPT_FILE="$PROMPT_BUILD"
MAX_ITERATIONS=0
WORK_DESCRIPTION=""
MODEL="$DEFAULT_MODEL"
VERBOSE=""
NO_PUSH=true
STOP_ON_COMPLETE=true

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        -h|--help)
            show_usage
            exit 0
            ;;
        -m|--model)
            MODEL="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        -v|--verbose)
            VERBOSE="--verbose"
            shift
            ;;
        --no-push)
            NO_PUSH=true
            shift
            ;;
        --no-stop-on-complete)
            STOP_ON_COMPLETE=false
            shift
            ;;
        --raw)
            PRETTY_OUTPUT=false
            shift
            ;;
        plan)
            MODE="plan"
            PROMPT_FILE="$PROMPT_PLAN"
            shift
            if [[ $# -gt 0 && "$1" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
                MAX_ITERATIONS=$1
                shift
            fi
            ;;
        plan-work)
            MODE="plan-work"
            PROMPT_FILE="$PROMPT_PLAN_WORK"
            shift
            if [[ $# -gt 0 && ! "$1" =~ ^- ]]; then
                WORK_DESCRIPTION="$1"
                shift
            else
                log_error "plan-work requires a work description"
                echo "Usage: ./loop.sh plan-work \"description of work\""
                exit 1
            fi
            if [[ $# -gt 0 && "$1" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
                MAX_ITERATIONS=$1
                shift
            else
                MAX_ITERATIONS=5
            fi
            ;;
        [0-9]*)
            MAX_ITERATIONS=$1
            shift
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown option: $1"
            show_usage
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# =============================================================================
# VALIDATION
# =============================================================================

check_prerequisites

if [[ ! -f "$PROMPT_FILE" ]]; then
    log_error "Prompt file not found: $PROMPT_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ "$MODE" == "plan-work" ]]; then
    CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
    if [[ "$CURRENT_BRANCH" == "main" || "$CURRENT_BRANCH" == "master" ]]; then
        log_error "plan-work should be run on a work branch, not main/master"
        echo "Create a work branch first: git checkout -b ralph/your-work"
        exit 1
    fi
    export WORK_SCOPE="$WORK_DESCRIPTION"
fi

# Initialize circuit breaker
if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
    cb_init
    log_info "Circuit breaker initialized (cost limit: \$${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999})"
fi

# =============================================================================
# MAIN LOOP
# =============================================================================

print_banner

CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
ITERATION=0
START_TIME=$(date +%s)

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
log_info "Mode:       ${YELLOW}$MODE${NC}"
log_info "Branch:     ${CYAN}$CURRENT_BRANCH${NC}"
log_info "Prompt:     $PROMPT_FILE"
log_info "Model:      $MODEL"
[[ $MAX_ITERATIONS -gt 0 ]] && log_info "Max:        $MAX_ITERATIONS iterations"
[[ -n "$WORK_DESCRIPTION" ]] && log_info "Work:       $WORK_DESCRIPTION"
log_info "Circuit:    $([[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]] && echo "ENABLED" || echo "DISABLED")"
log_info "Auto-stop:  $([[ "$STOP_ON_COMPLETE" == "true" ]] && echo "on completion signal" || echo "manual only")"
log_info "Output:     $([[ "$PRETTY_OUTPUT" == "true" && -x "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT" ]] && echo "formatted" || echo "raw JSON")"
log_info "Timeouts:   Bash=${BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS}ms API=${API_TIMEOUT_MS}ms"
if [[ "$NO_PUSH" == "true" ]]; then
    log_warn "NO_PUSH=true: Work is LOCAL ONLY. Run 'git push' manually or set NO_PUSH=false"
fi
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo ""

# Trap for cleanup
trap 'echo ""; log_warn "Loop interrupted by user"; cleanup; exit 130' INT TERM

while true; do
    # Check iteration limit
    if [[ $MAX_ITERATIONS -gt 0 && $ITERATION -ge $MAX_ITERATIONS ]]; then
        log_success "Reached max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS"
        
        if [[ "$MODE" == "plan-work" ]]; then
            echo ""
            echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
            log_success "Scoped plan created for: $WORK_DESCRIPTION"
            log_info "To build, run: ./loop.sh 50"
            echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
        fi
        break
    fi

    # Check rate limit
    if ! check_rate_limit; then
        log_error "Rate limit exceeded. Stopping."
        break
    fi

    # Check circuit breaker
    if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
        CB_RESULT=$(cb_check_circuit 2>&1) || {
            log_error "Circuit breaker OPEN: $CB_RESULT"
            echo ""
            cb_status
            echo ""
            log_info "To reset: ./circuit-breaker.sh reset"
            break
        }
    fi

    ITERATION=$((ITERATION + 1))
    ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START_TIME))
    OUTPUT_FILE="$OUTPUT_DIR/iteration-$ITERATION.json"
    
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}  ITERATION $ITERATION  │  Elapsed: ${ELAPSED}s  │  Branch: $CURRENT_BRANCH${NC}"
    if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
        cb_state=$(cb_get_state 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')
        cb_cost=$(echo "$cb_state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost // 0' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
        echo -e "${BLUE}  Est. Cost: \$${cb_cost}  │  Limit: \$${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999}${NC}"
    fi
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo ""

    # ==========================================================================
    # CORE RALPH LOOP - Clean Context on Each Iteration
    # ==========================================================================

    # Run pre-iteration hook (informational warnings)
    if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/hooks/pre-iteration.sh" ]]; then
        source "$SCRIPT_DIR/hooks/pre-iteration.sh" || true
    fi

    ITER_START=$(date +%s)

    # Run Claude with retry logic
    run_claude_with_retry "$PROMPT_FILE" "$OUTPUT_FILE" "$MODE"

    ITER_END=$(date +%s)
    ITER_DURATION=$((ITER_END - ITER_START))
    
    # Record call for rate limiting
    increment_call_count

    # Record iteration for circuit breaker
    if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
        cb_record_iteration
    fi

    # Check for errors in output
    if ERROR_MSG=$(check_for_errors "$OUTPUT_FILE"); then
        log_warn "Error detected: $ERROR_MSG"
        if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
            cb_record_error "$ERROR_MSG"
        fi
    fi

    # Check for completion signals
    if [[ "$STOP_ON_COMPLETE" == "true" ]]; then
        if SIGNAL=$(check_completion_signal "$OUTPUT_FILE"); then
            log_success "Completion signal detected: $SIGNAL"
            
            # Push final changes before exit
            if [[ "$NO_PUSH" == "false" ]]; then
                CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
                push_changes "$CURRENT_BRANCH" || log_warn "Final push failed"
            fi
            
            break
        fi
    fi

    # Push changes
    if [[ "$NO_PUSH" == "false" ]]; then
        CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
        if ! push_changes "$CURRENT_BRANCH"; then
            log_error "CRITICAL: Cannot push to remote after 3 retries."
            log_error "Work is only local! Stopping to prevent data loss."
            log_info "Fix credentials and run: git push origin $CURRENT_BRANCH"
            break
        fi
    fi

    log_success "Iteration $ITERATION completed (${ITER_DURATION}s)"
done

# =============================================================================
# SUMMARY
# =============================================================================

cleanup

TOTAL_TIME=$(($(date +%s) - START_TIME))
echo ""
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
log_success "Ralph loop completed!"
log_info "Total iterations: $ITERATION"
log_info "Total time: ${TOTAL_TIME}s"
log_info "Avg time/iteration: $((TOTAL_TIME / (ITERATION > 0 ? ITERATION : 1)))s"

if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
    echo ""
    cb_status
fi

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# =============================================================================
     3→# format-output.sh - Human-readable output formatting for Ralph loop monitoring
     4→# =============================================================================
     5→# Transforms Claude CLI stream-json output into readable format.
     6→#
     7→# Usage:
     8→#   claude -p ... --output-format=stream-json | ./format-output.sh
     9→#   cat claude-output.json | ./format-output.sh
    10→#
    11→# See: specs/RALPH_OUTPUT_FORMAT.md for requirements
    12→# =============================================================================
    13→
    14→set -uo pipefail
    15→
    16→# Colors
    17→RED='\033[0;31m'
    18→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    19→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    20→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    21→CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    22→DIM='\033[2m'
    23→BOLD='\033[1m'
    24→NC='\033[0m'
    25→
    26→# Icons (from spec)
    27→ICON_TEXT="💬"
    28→ICON_TOOL="🔧"
    29→ICON_THINK="🧠"
    30→ICON_TODO="📋"
    31→ICON_ERROR="❌"
    32→ICON_COMMIT="📦"
    33→
    34→# Configuration
    35→MAX_LINE_WIDTH=${MAX_LINE_WIDTH:-120}
    36→SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC=${SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC:-300}  # 5 minutes
    37→RAW_OUTPUT_FILE=${RAW_OUTPUT_FILE:-}  # Optional: save raw JSON
    38→
    39→# State tracking
    40→START_TIME=$(date +%s)
    41→LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$START_TIME
    42→LINE_COUNT=0
    43→ERROR_COUNT=0
    44→TOOL_COUNT=0
    45→TEXT_COUNT=0
    46→COMMIT_COUNT=0
    47→TODO_COMPLETED=0
    48→
    49→# =============================================================================
    50→# HELPER FUNCTIONS
    51→# =============================================================================
    52→
    53→timestamp() {
    54→    date '+[%H:%M:%S]'
    55→}
    56→
    57→truncate_line() {
    58→    local text="$1"
    59→    local max_len="${2:-$MAX_LINE_WIDTH}"
    60→    if [[ ${#text} -gt $max_len ]]; then
    61→        echo "${text:0:$((max_len - 3))}..."
    62→    else
    63→        echo "$text"
    64→    fi
    65→}
    66→
    67→format_elapsed() {
    68→    local seconds="$1"
    69→    local hours=$((seconds / 3600))
    70→    local minutes=$(( (seconds % 3600) / 60 ))
    71→    local secs=$((seconds % 60))
    72→
    73→    if [[ $hours -gt 0 ]]; then
    74→        printf "%dh %02dm" "$hours" "$minutes"
    75→    elif [[ $minutes -gt 0 ]]; then
    76→        printf "%dm %02ds" "$minutes" "$secs"
    77→    else
    78→        printf "%ds" "$secs"
    79→    fi
    80→}
    81→
    82→print_summary() {
    83→    local now
    84→    now=$(date +%s)
    85→    local elapsed=$((now - START_TIME))
    86→
    87→    echo ""
    88→    echo -e "${BLUE}═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    89→    echo -e "${BLUE} Elapsed: $(format_elapsed "$elapsed") | Tools: $TOOL_COUNT | Errors: $ERROR_COUNT${NC}"
    90→    echo -e "${BLUE} Commits: $COMMIT_COUNT | Todos Done: $TODO_COMPLETED${NC}"
    91→    echo -e "${BLUE}═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    92→    echo ""
    93→
    94→    LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$now
    95→}
    96→
    97→check_summary_needed() {
    98→    local now
    99→    now=$(date +%s)
   100→    if [[ $((now - LAST_SUMMARY_TIME)) -ge $SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC ]]; then
   101→        print_summary
   102→    fi
   103→}
   104→
   105→# =============================================================================
   106→# MESSAGE FORMATTERS
   107→# =============================================================================
   108→
   109→format_text_message() {
   110→    local text="$1"
   111→
   112→    # Skip empty or whitespace-only messages
   113→    [[ -z "${text// /}" ]] && return
   114→
   115→    TEXT_COUNT=$((TEXT_COUNT + 1))
   116→
   117→    # Check for commit-related messages
   118→    if [[ "$text" =~ (committed|commit\ [a-f0-9]{7,}|git\ commit) ]]; then
   119→        COMMIT_COUNT=$((COMMIT_COUNT + 1))
   120→        local short_msg
   121→        short_msg=$(truncate_line "$text")
   122→        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${GREEN}${ICON_COMMIT}${NC} ${GREEN}$short_msg${NC}"
   123→    else
   124→        # Regular text message
   125→        local short_msg
   126→        short_msg=$(truncate_line "$text")
   127→        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${ICON_TEXT} $short_msg"
   128→    fi
   129→}
   130→
   131→format_tool_use() {
   132→    local tool_name="$1"
   133→    local tool_input="$2"
   134→
   135→    TOOL_COUNT=$((TOOL_COUNT + 1))
   136→
   137→    local description=""
   138→
   139→    case "$tool_name" in
   140→        Read)
   141→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.file_path // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   142→            description="Read: $description"
   143→            ;;
   144→        Write)
   145→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.file_path // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   146→            description="Write: $description"
   147→            ;;
   148→        Edit)
   149→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.file_path // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   150→            description="Edit: $description"
   151→            ;;
   152→        Bash)
   153→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.description // .command[:60] // "command"' 2>/dev/null)
   154→            description="Bash: $description"
   155→            ;;
   156→        Glob)
   157→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.pattern // "pattern"' 2>/dev/null)
   158→            description="Glob: $description"
   159→            ;;
   160→        Grep)
   161→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.pattern // "pattern"' 2>/dev/null)
   162→            description="Grep: $description"
   163→            ;;
   164→        Task)
   165→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.description // "task"' 2>/dev/null)
   166→            description="Task: $description"
   167→            ;;
   168→        TodoWrite)
   169→            # Extract todo status summary
   170→            local completed pending in_progress
   171→            completed=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="completed")] | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   172→            pending=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="pending")] | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   173→            in_progress=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="in_progress")] | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   174→            TODO_COMPLETED=$completed
   175→            description="Todos: $in_progress active, $completed done, $pending pending"
   176→
   177→            # Print todo update with special formatting
   178→            echo -e "$(timestamp) ${YELLOW}${ICON_TODO}${NC} ${YELLOW}$description${NC}"
   179→            return
   180→            ;;
   181→        WebFetch|WebSearch)
   182→            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.url // .query // "web"' 2>/dev/null)
   183→            description="$tool_name: $description"
   184→            ;;
   185→        mcp__playwright__*)
   186→            local action="${tool_name#mcp__playwright__}"
   187→            description="Playwright: $action"
   188→            ;;
   189→        *)
   190→            description="$tool_name"
   191→            ;;
   192→    esac
   193→
   194→    description=$(truncate_line "$description")
   195→    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${CYAN}${ICON_TOOL}${NC} ${CYAN}$description${NC}"
   196→}
   197→
   198→format_thinking() {
   199→    local thinking="$1"
   200→
   201→    # Get first line, truncated
   202→    local first_line
   203→    first_line=$(echo "$thinking" | head -n 1)
   204→    first_line=$(truncate_line "$first_line" 80)
   205→
   206→    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${DIM}${ICON_THINK} $first_line${NC}"
   207→}
   208→
   209→format_error() {
   210→    local error_msg="$1"
   211→
   212→    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
   213→
   214→    # Errors get full output (not truncated) and red color
   215→    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${RED}${ICON_ERROR} ERROR: $error_msg${NC}" >&2
   216→}
   217→
   218→format_tool_result() {
   219→    local tool_name="$1"
   220→    local result="$2"
   221→    local is_error="$3"
   222→
   223→    if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
   224→        local error_msg
   225→        error_msg=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.error // .message // .' 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)

   227→    fi
   228→    # Successful tool results are not displayed (too verbose)
   229→}
   230→
   231→# =============================================================================
   232→# CONTENT FILTERS
   233→# =============================================================================
   234→
   235→should_skip_line() {
   236→    local line="$1"
   237→
   238→    # Skip empty lines
   239→    [[ -z "$line" ]] && return 0
   240→
   241→    # Skip if not valid JSON (passthrough non-JSON)
   242→    if ! echo "$line" | jq -e '.' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   243→        return 1  # Don't skip - print as-is
   244→    fi
   245→
   246→    local type
   247→    type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   248→
   249→    # Skip these message types (internal/noisy)
   250→    case "$type" in
   251→        usage|system|ping|init|session|metadata|cache_*)
   252→            return 0  # Skip
   253→            ;;
   254→    esac
   255→
   256→    # Skip lines with only UUIDs, session IDs
   257→    if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*\{[[:space:]]*\"(id|session_id|uuid)\" ]]; then

   259→    fi

   263→        return 0
   264→    fi
   265→
   266→    return 1  # Process the line
   267→}
   268→
   269→# =============================================================================
   270→# MAIN PROCESSOR
   271→# =============================================================================
   272→
   273→process_line() {
   274→    local line="$1"
   275→
   276→    LINE_COUNT=$((LINE_COUNT + 1))
   277→
   278→    # Save raw output if configured
   279→    if [[ -n "$RAW_OUTPUT_FILE" ]]; then

   281→    fi
   282→
   283→    # Check if line should be skipped
   284→    if should_skip_line "$line"; then

   286→    fi
   287→
   288→    # Try to parse as JSON
   289→    if ! echo "$line" | jq -e '.' >/dev/null 2>&1; then

   291→        echo "$line"

   293→    fi
   294→
   295→    local type
   296→    type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   297→
   298→    case "$type" in
   299→        assistant)

   301→            local content_count
   302→            content_count=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.message.content | length' 2>/dev/null)

   304→            if [[ "$content_count" -gt 0 ]]; then

   306→                for ((i=0; i<content_count; i++)); do
   307→                    local block_type block_json
   308→                    block_json=$(echo "$line" | jq -r ".message.content[$i]" 2>/dev/null)
   309→                    block_type=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   310→
   311→                    case "$block_type" in
   312→                        text)

   314→                            text=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.text // ""' 2>/dev/null)
   315→                            format_text_message "$text"
   316→                            ;;
   317→                        tool_use)
   318→                            local tool_name tool_input
   319→                            tool_name=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.name // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   320→                            tool_input=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.input // {}' 2>/dev/null)
   321→                            format_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
   322→                            ;;
   323→                        thinking)
   324→                            local thinking
   325→                            thinking=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.thinking // ""' 2>/dev/null)
   326→                            format_thinking "$thinking"
   327→                            ;;
   328→                    esac
   329→                done
   330→            fi
   331→            ;;
   332→
   333→        user)
   334→            # User messages contain tool_result typically
   335→            local content_count
   336→            content_count=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.message.content | length' 2>/dev/null)
   337→
   338→            if [[ "$content_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
   339→                for ((i=0; i<content_count; i++)); do
   340→                    local block_type block_json
   341→                    block_json=$(echo "$line" | jq -r ".message.content[$i]" 2>/dev/null)
   342→                    block_type=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
   343→
   344→                    if [[ "$block_type" == "tool_result" ]]; then
   345→                        local is_error
   346→                        is_error=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.is_error // false' 2>/dev/null)
   347→                        if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
   348→                            local error_content
   349→                            error_content=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.content // "Tool error"' 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)
   350→                            format_error "Tool result: $error_content"
   351→                        fi
   352→                        # Skip successful tool results (too verbose)
   353→                    fi
   354→                done
   355→            fi
   356→            ;;
   357→

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# format-output.sh - Human-readable output formatting for Ralph loop monitoring
# =============================================================================
# Transforms Claude CLI stream-json output into readable format.
#
# Usage:
#   claude -p ... --output-format=stream-json | ./format-output.sh
#   cat claude-output.json | ./format-output.sh
#
# See: specs/RALPH_OUTPUT_FORMAT.md for requirements
# =============================================================================

set -uo pipefail

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
DIM='\033[2m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'

# Icons (from spec)
ICON_TEXT="💬"
ICON_TOOL="🔧"
ICON_THINK="🧠"
ICON_TODO="📋"
ICON_ERROR="❌"
ICON_COMMIT="📦"

# Configuration
MAX_LINE_WIDTH=${MAX_LINE_WIDTH:-120}
SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC=${SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC:-300}  # 5 minutes
RAW_OUTPUT_FILE=${RAW_OUTPUT_FILE:-}  # Optional: save raw JSON

# State tracking
START_TIME=$(date +%s)
LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$START_TIME
LINE_COUNT=0
ERROR_COUNT=0
TOOL_COUNT=0
TEXT_COUNT=0
COMMIT_COUNT=0
TODO_COMPLETED=0

# =============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

timestamp() {
    date '+[%H:%M:%S]'
}

truncate_line() {
    local text="$1"
    local max_len="${2:-$MAX_LINE_WIDTH}"
    if [[ ${#text} -gt $max_len ]]; then
        echo "${text:0:$((max_len - 3))}..."
    else
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

format_elapsed() {
    local seconds="$1"
    local hours=$((seconds / 3600))
    local minutes=$(( (seconds % 3600) / 60 ))
    local secs=$((seconds % 60))

    if [[ $hours -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf "%dh %02dm" "$hours" "$minutes"
    elif [[ $minutes -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf "%dm %02ds" "$minutes" "$secs"
    else
        printf "%ds" "$secs"
    fi
}

print_summary() {
    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    local elapsed=$((now - START_TIME))

    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE} Elapsed: $(format_elapsed "$elapsed") | Tools: $TOOL_COUNT | Errors: $ERROR_COUNT${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE} Commits: $COMMIT_COUNT | Todos Done: $TODO_COMPLETED${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo ""

    LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$now
}

check_summary_needed() {
    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    if [[ $((now - LAST_SUMMARY_TIME)) -ge $SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC ]]; then
        print_summary
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# MESSAGE FORMATTERS
# =============================================================================

format_text_message() {
    local text="$1"

    # Skip empty or whitespace-only messages
    [[ -z "${text// /}" ]] && return

    TEXT_COUNT=$((TEXT_COUNT + 1))

    # Check for commit-related messages
    if [[ "$text" =~ (committed|commit\ [a-f0-9]{7,}|git\ commit) ]]; then
        COMMIT_COUNT=$((COMMIT_COUNT + 1))
        local short_msg
        short_msg=$(truncate_line "$text")
        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${GREEN}${ICON_COMMIT}${NC} ${GREEN}$short_msg${NC}"
    else
        # Regular text message
        local short_msg
        short_msg=$(truncate_line "$text")
        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${ICON_TEXT} $short_msg"
    fi
}

format_tool_use() {
    local tool_name="$1"
    local tool_input="$2"

    TOOL_COUNT=$((TOOL_COUNT + 1))

    local description=""

    case "$tool_name" in
        Read)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.file_path // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Read: $description"
            ;;
        Write)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.file_path // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Write: $description"
            ;;
        Edit)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.file_path // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Edit: $description"
            ;;
        Bash)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.description // .command[:60] // "command"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Bash: $description"
            ;;
        Glob)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.pattern // "pattern"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Glob: $description"
            ;;
        Grep)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.pattern // "pattern"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Grep: $description"
            ;;
        Task)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.description // "task"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Task: $description"
            ;;
        TodoWrite)
            # Extract todo status summary
            local completed pending in_progress
            completed=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="completed")] | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            pending=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="pending")] | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            in_progress=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="in_progress")] | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            TODO_COMPLETED=$completed
            description="Todos: $in_progress active, $completed done, $pending pending"

            # Print todo update with special formatting
            echo -e "$(timestamp) ${YELLOW}${ICON_TODO}${NC} ${YELLOW}$description${NC}"
            return
            ;;
        WebFetch|WebSearch)
            description=$(echo "$tool_input" | jq -r '.url // .query // "web"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="$tool_name: $description"
            ;;
        mcp__playwright__*)
            local action="${tool_name#mcp__playwright__}"
            description="Playwright: $action"
            ;;
        *)
            description="$tool_name"
            ;;
    esac

    description=$(truncate_line "$description")
    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${CYAN}${ICON_TOOL}${NC} ${CYAN}$description${NC}"
}

format_thinking() {
    local thinking="$1"

    # Get first line, truncated
    local first_line
    first_line=$(echo "$thinking" | head -n 1)
    first_line=$(truncate_line "$first_line" 80)

    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${DIM}${ICON_THINK} $first_line${NC}"
}

format_error() {
    local error_msg="$1"

    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))

    # Errors get full output (not truncated) and red color
    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${RED}${ICON_ERROR} ERROR: $error_msg${NC}" >&2
}

format_tool_result() {
    local tool_name="$1"
    local result="$2"
    local is_error="$3"

    if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.error // .message // .' 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)
        format_error "Tool $tool_name failed: $error_msg"
    fi
    # Successful tool results are not displayed (too verbose)
}

# =============================================================================
# CONTENT FILTERS
# =============================================================================

should_skip_line() {
    local line="$1"

    # Skip empty lines
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && return 0

    # Skip if not valid JSON (passthrough non-JSON)
    if ! echo "$line" | jq -e '.' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 1  # Don't skip - print as-is
    fi

    local type
    type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)

    # Skip these message types (internal/noisy)
    case "$type" in
        usage|system|ping|init|session|metadata|cache_*)
            return 0  # Skip
            ;;
    esac

    # Skip lines with only UUIDs, session IDs
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*\{[[:space:]]*\"(id|session_id|uuid)\" ]]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Skip base64 content detection
    if echo "$line" | jq -e 'select(.content | type == "string") | .content | test("^[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{100,}$")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 0
    fi

    return 1  # Process the line
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN PROCESSOR
# =============================================================================

process_line() {
    local line="$1"

    LINE_COUNT=$((LINE_COUNT + 1))

    # Save raw output if configured
    if [[ -n "$RAW_OUTPUT_FILE" ]]; then
        echo "$line" >> "$RAW_OUTPUT_FILE"
    fi

    # Check if line should be skipped
    if should_skip_line "$line"; then
        return
    fi

    # Try to parse as JSON
    if ! echo "$line" | jq -e '.' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # Not JSON - print as-is (likely startup messages)
        echo "$line"
        return
    fi

    local type
    type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)

    case "$type" in
        assistant)
            # Claude CLI wraps content in .message.content[] array
            local content_count
            content_count=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.message.content | length' 2>/dev/null)

            if [[ "$content_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
                # Process each content block in the array
                for ((i=0; i<content_count; i++)); do
                    local block_type block_json
                    block_json=$(echo "$line" | jq -r ".message.content[$i]" 2>/dev/null)
                    block_type=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)

                    case "$block_type" in
                        text)
                            local text
                            text=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.text // ""' 2>/dev/null)
                            format_text_message "$text"
                            ;;
                        tool_use)
                            local tool_name tool_input
                            tool_name=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.name // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
                            tool_input=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.input // {}' 2>/dev/null)
                            format_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
                            ;;
                        thinking)
                            local thinking
                            thinking=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.thinking // ""' 2>/dev/null)
                            format_thinking "$thinking"
                            ;;
                    esac
                done
            fi
            ;;

        user)
            # User messages contain tool_result typically
            local content_count
            content_count=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.message.content | length' 2>/dev/null)

            if [[ "$content_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
                for ((i=0; i<content_count; i++)); do
                    local block_type block_json
                    block_json=$(echo "$line" | jq -r ".message.content[$i]" 2>/dev/null)
                    block_type=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)

                    if [[ "$block_type" == "tool_result" ]]; then
                        local is_error
                        is_error=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.is_error // false' 2>/dev/null)
                        if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
                            local error_content
                            error_content=$(echo "$block_json" | jq -r '.content // "Tool error"' 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)
                            format_error "Tool result: $error_content"
                        fi
                        # Skip successful tool results (too verbose)
                    fi
                done
            fi
            ;;

        text)
            local text
            text=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.text // .content // ""' 2>/dev/null)
            format_text_message "$text"
            ;;

        tool_use)
            local tool_name tool_input
            tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.name // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
            tool_input=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.input // {}' 2>/dev/null)
            format_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
            ;;

        tool_result)
            local tool_name result is_error
            tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.tool_use_id // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
            result=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.content // ""' 2>/dev/null)
            is_error=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.is_error // false' 2>/dev/null)
            format_tool_result "$tool_name" "$result" "$is_error"
            ;;

        thinking)
            local thinking
            thinking=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.thinking // ""' 2>/dev/null)
            format_thinking "$thinking"
            ;;

        error)
            local error_msg
            error_msg=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.error.message // .error // .message // "Unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
            format_error "$error_msg"
            ;;

        content_block_start)
            local block_type
            block_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.content_block.type // ""' 2>/dev/null)
            if [[ "$block_type" == "tool_use" ]]; then
                local tool_name tool_input
                tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.content_block.name // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
                tool_input=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.content_block.input // {}' 2>/dev/null)
                format_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
            fi
            ;;

        content_block_delta)
            local delta_type delta_text
            delta_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.delta.type // ""' 2>/dev/null)
            case "$delta_type" in
                text_delta)
                    # Stream text - accumulate (for real streaming, would need buffering)
                    ;;
                thinking_delta)
                    # Thinking delta - skip (too verbose)
                    ;;
            esac
            ;;

        message_start|message_delta|message_stop|content_block_stop)
            # Lifecycle events - skip
            ;;

        result)
            # Final result - check for commit-like patterns
            local text
            text=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.result // ""' 2>/dev/null)
            if [[ -n "$text" ]]; then
                format_text_message "$text"
            fi
            ;;

        *)
            # Unknown type - show in dim if it has meaningful content
            local content
            content=$(echo "$line" | jq -r 'to_entries | map(select(.key != "type")) | from_entries | if . == {} then empty else . end' 2>/dev/null)
            if [[ -n "$content" && "$content" != "null" ]]; then
                echo -e "$(timestamp) ${DIM}[$type]${NC}"
            fi
            ;;
    esac

    # Periodic summary check
    check_summary_needed
}

main() {
    # Check for jq
    if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - passing through raw output" >&2
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} Install jq for formatted output: brew install jq (macOS) / apt install jq (Linux)" >&2
        cat  # Passthrough mode
        exit 0
    fi

    # Process stdin line by line
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        process_line "$line"
    done

    # Final summary
    print_summary
}

# Run if executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi
